title: PR-flow rollout mechanism — per-lane dev-server ports, clone scope, landing gate, branch-protection posture
summary: Prep research for decision #1996 (the rollout mechanism under #1985 Rung 2's ratified adopt-PR-flow direction; extends the automated-writer-isolation-model theme; report we:reports/2026-06-30-pr-flow-rollout-mechanism.md). IN-REPO GROUNDING: the #1933 clone/lane/push machinery is RESOLVED and proven — persistent --reference-shared lane pool under ~/workspace/.lanes/<repo>/ (we:scripts/lane-pool.mjs), lane/* push + central merge-one-at-a-time-with-gate + rebase-retry, cross-repo coupled-clone dispatch (#1943); #1995 made the lane push reliable. What is NOT built is per-clone DEV SERVERS: WE vite is :3000 (we:vite.config.mts:113), the 11ty proxy targets are all hardcoded to localhost:8080 (we:vite.config.mts:119-153, a hand-maintained allowlist #210), 11ty is --port=8080 in package.json dev. KEY FINDING: FUI is NOT a port problem — it resolves by a RELATIVE path-alias to ../frontierui (we:vite.config.mts:15,206), so a coupled WE+FUI sibling clone (already how #1943 provisions cross-repo lanes) wires FUI for free; the entire unsolved coupling reduces to one pair of ports (vite + 11ty) plus one hardcoded cross-reference (the proxy target). PRIOR ART (dev-infra): Vite's idiomatic shape reads server.port + proxy.target from env via loadEnv with strictPort:true (fail-loud on collision) and changeOrigin:true (vite.dev/config/server-options); deterministic offset/index gives stable addressable URLs with no registry while dynamic free-port scan (get-port/portfinder) needs a registry/lockfile and loses run-to-run addressability; monorepo/worktree convention is a hash-or-index -> reserved port-BLOCK per worktree injected via a generated .env.local, with linear-probe on collision (Superset *_PORT_BASE blocks; portree FNV32(branch:service)%range + probe; github.com/fairy-pitta/portree); Docker-compose lesson: isolating NAMES != isolating host PORTS, drive host ports from a generated per-instance env by script not by hand; keep the upstream (11ty) port internal behind the proxy, only the target host:port is parameterized. SYNTHESIS (5 forks + 3 support-both; skeptic-revised in prep, then DECIDED IN REVIEW WITH THE OPERATOR 2026-06-30): Fork 1 clone-scope = MERIT FORK, decided ISOLATE-BY-DEFAULT FOR AUTOMATION — every agent/workflow writing session works in a clone and converges via the integrator's auto-merge (main is convergence-only for agents), while the HUMAN keeps direct-to-main as the single trusted writer; the prepared 'tiered/file-overlap' default was refuted (the file-overlap line is only computable for the orchestrator's own partition, never for an ad-hoc stranger writing main, so it can't guarantee parallel safety, and it doesn't practice the PR-flow #1985 ratified); Fork 2 per-lane port assignment = CODE-SHAPE FORK, RATIFIED pure deterministic-offset + strictPort (no linear probe — a probe IS dynamic discovery and dissolves the fork) over dynamic-scan+registry, with env-driven ports + proxy target via a generated .env.local, PLUS per-repo thousands-bands: WE 3000+/8080+, plateau-app 4000+, Frontier UI 6000+ (5000/7000 are macOS-reserved — AirPlay/ControlCenter, verified taken; 6000 verified free; FUI's current 3001/3002/8082 overlap WE's band and relocate to 6000s as part of the build); Fork 3 landing gate = MERIT FORK, decided FULLY AUTOMATIC auto-merge-on-gate-green — no manual merge, no human review (operator: 'I don't want to have to manual merge or test anything'); the #1895 render gap is closed by automation not a human, and the MECHANISM for that is Fork 5 (not a human review step); per-item human review stays opt-in but never required; Fork 4 branch-protection = MERIT FORK, decided ASYMMETRIC — main writable by the human, observe-only for AI/agents (agents PR-flow only); enforcement is convention now + a bot-principal branch rule later; full observe-only stays a future flip when a second human appears; Fork 5 how-visual-changes-land-safely = MERIT FORK (reclassified in review from a validation-gate — with Fork 3 taking no human gate, this is a forced design either/or, not a 'build the harness, when?' prioritization): recommended default (a) RENDER-CHECK IN THE GATE — the per-lane visual harness is a v1 deliverable, a visual-touching lane auto-merges only when a headless Playwright render check passes (still no human); fallback (b) agents don't auto-land visual changes, the human owns visual surfaces via the Fork-4 direct-main path; the do-nothing branch (auto-land unverified) is broken (defeats the gate); acceptance test = a lane boots WE+FUI cross-origin and headless Playwright reproduces the #1895 transparent-.fui-card regression AND its fix from the CLI. The support-both consequences (per-clone dev server/HMR, proxy-target env parameterization, FUI sibling-clone wiring) are not forks.
